I ain't wearin’ handcuffs.

You put those on or you stop worryin'

Ruth "a 1ook", then bends down and puts on the

All four men know how to handle horses.

The brutal wind gets more brutal still.

INT - MINNIE'S STABLE - DAY (21)

The stable is a rather large affair. It houses eighteen horses.
Six from John Ruth's coach. Six from that other coach out there
that brought Oswaldo and his group. As well as six other horses
that would of replaced one of those wagons if there hadn't

been a blizzard.

It's quite a full house.
The four men get the six horses in to their stalis.
As they finish, Bob the Mexican says to the other three;
BOB
I'll feed and water the horses. You go

inside and get some hot coffy. I've got
some stew cooking. Should be done soon.

JOE GAGE
My mother. I'm a cow puncher. I just
finished a big long drive. I wasn't
just an ass in a saddle this time,
either. I was partners on this one.
For once in my iife I made a pretty penny.
I was coming here to spend Christmas
with mother.
JOHN RUTH
Really?

JOE GAGE
Really.

JOHN RUTH

Funny, you don't look like the coming
home for Christmas type.

CHRIS
(CON'T)
Cap't.Chris Mannix, Mannix Marauders.

Gen.Smithers returns the salute.

GEN .SMITHERS
Erskine’s boy?

CHRIS
Yes sir.

Chris points to the empty cozy chair covered in animal skins,
across From The 01d General.

CHRIS
(CON'T)
May IT sit down, sir?

Instead of cordially inviting the young respectful man to sit
down, the Old Man says curtly;

GEN-SMITHERS
According to the Yankees, it's a free
country. Do what you want.

(placing his hand on

Smithers shoulder)
I guarantee I will hang you by the neck
until you are dead, once we arrive in
Red Rack.

Chris Mannix looks over at Maj.Warren and says;

CHRIS
I guarantee that too.

John Ruth chimes in over by the bar é6rinking with Domergue.

JOHN RUTH
Yeah Warren, that the problem with old
men. You can kick ‘em down the stairs
and say it's a accident, but ya' can't
shoot ’em.

OSWALDO
Now gentlemen, since we may be trapped
here close together like for a few days,
may I suggest a possible solution?

Domergue, however, hasn't moved from her
spot at the community dinner table since
John Ruth uncuffed her.

The CAMPRA begins to CRANE DOWN towards Domergue till it LANDS on
@ TIGHT MEDIOM of her.

NWIRRATOR (VO)
At this point in the story Daisy Domergue
is keen with anticipation. Something
her face, body, and demeanor are trying
very hard to conceal. Because, as stated
in the title of this section of Chapter
three, Domergue's got a secret.

FREEZE FRAME on PDomergue.

NARRATOR (VO)
Let's go back a bit.

cuT TO

OSWALDO
Because his coffy was dreadful.

{to Bob)
Really? Weil ain't that interesting.

BOB

(to Maj.Warren)
You @idn't have any of my coffy,
bounty hunter. So don't be so sure about
what this little man says.

JOE GAGE
I hag his coffy. Wasn't the best coffy
I ever drank, but wasn't nothin' wrong
with it.

BOB
If you want me to make a pot of coffy,
all you have to do is ask?

GEMMA

Really? You wanna’ buy me jelly beans?

OSWALDO
If I may be so bold.

The way he talks makes her giggle. Her pretty smile gets even
wider.

CHRIS

{to Domergue)
Your gonna' make a deal with me? I just
shot your brother?

DOMERGUE
Yeah, but it was just his arm, and you
didn't know what you were doin’.

CHRIS
What if he's gotta' dead arm now?

DOMERGUE
Still, you didn't mean it - And it's
his left arm anyway. And it looks like
Warren killed 'em anyway. And I'm gonna’
probably lose my leg. Look, this
only works if we're all reasonable.
Now with my brother gut shot on that floor,
I'm in charge of this gang,
